behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names distribute.ts, actionDistribute.tsx, textWysiwyg.tsx, updateBoundElements, Scene type, scene.mutateElement, simultaneouslyUpdated: group, and app.scene — all literals tested.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction essentially dictates the fix: 'Elements are mutated via scene.mutateElement instead of newElementWith', 'updateBoundElements(element, scene, { simultaneouslyUpdated: group })', 'updateBoundElements(container, app.scene)' after mutateElement — these are direct implementation prescriptions.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert specific strings like 'scene.mutateElement', 'updateBoundElements(element, scene', 'simultaneouslyUpdated: group', and regex on exact mutateElement+updateBoundElements sequence; an alternative valid fix (e.g., passing appState differently or using a helper) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests include subprocess execution of yarn test:typecheck, test:code, test:app running actual unit tests alongside text-grep tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: Repo test suites (distribute, binding, wysiwyg tests) execute real behavior and would fail with stub implementations.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Every behavior in instruction (imports, signature, mutateElement usage, updateBoundElements calls, caller passes scene, wysiwyg calls) has corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests have descriptive names and docstrings, with a commented section dividing pass-to-pass tests.

structured_data_schema.reason: Code task, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and names match the repo conventions.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names exact files (packages/element/src/binding.ts, actionDistribute.tsx), exact option keys ({ simultaneouslyUpdated: group }), and exact call signatures — localization is trivialized.
